facts.text: Noma traces its origins to 1882, when the area was first settled during a period of expansion in northern Holmes County.
Noma’s name is historically linked to the Noma Mill Company, a timber milling operation that played a significant role in the town’s early economy.
The community formally organized its local government when Noma was incorporated in July 1904, allowing residents to manage municipal affairs.
Lumber production drove settlement; the presence of a mill helped establish Noma as a recognized community during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
Today, Noma continues to serve as a quiet residential community in northern Holmes County, located just south of the Alabama state line.
Noma’s history is defined not by growth, but by resilience, continuity, and the lasting contributions of the people who have called it home for more than a century.
